title: The Macro Minute: Is it time to “sell America” again?

Happy Tuesday out there team42. It's
your skipper here at Darius Dell to
present our macro minute for Tuesday,
January 20th, 2026. As always, we'll
start with the executive summary from
today's leadoff morning note. So, let's
dive right in. Today's key macro
question is, is it time to sell America
again? The short answer is the growing
geopolitically driven supply demand
imbalance in the Treasury bond market is
likely to catalyze a further rotation
out of US assets this year, which is
precisely what should occur amid a
robust US and global growth backdrop.
Terms of analytical nuance, global risk
assets sold off as stocks, bonds, and
the dollar declined while Bitcoin sank
and gold printed record highs after
President Trump threatened tariffs on
multiple European countries. The latest
tariff threat comes ahead of senior
level talks at the World Economic Forum
in Davos, Switzerland, amid an
escalating confrontation tied to a
stated ambitions regarding Greenland. As
President Trump travels to Davos, he has
intensified clashes with European
leaders by pressuring the UK over plans
to return sovereignty of Diego Garcia
and Maitius uh threatening tariffs on
eight European countries opposing his
Greenland stance and attempting to
compel France to participate in his
broad proposed board of peace. Measured
through last week, average realized
volatility across US bonds, equities,
and the dollar over the prior month
failed to the lowest level since at
least 199 1990. That period of
exceptionally little calm appears uh now
appears to be broken as European leaders
debate how to counter President Trump
with some market partic
arguing renewed turbulence may be
necessary to back him off given the US's
president's focus on avoiding becoming a
lame duck in the upcoming midterm
elections this fall. The uptick in cross
asset volatility comes amid further
evidence of the historic degree of
crowded bullish positioning observed by
by our positioning model and other
indicators. According to Bank of
America's latest fund manager survey,
positioning turned the most bullish
since July of 2021, cash allocations are
at record lows, pushing the firm's bull
and bear indicator into what it labeled
hyperbull, which is a signal to
accumulate hedges and safe havens. This
comes uh as nearly 50% of respondents
reported having no downside equity
protection, which was the highest share
since 2018.
Addressing earlier reports that Europe
could sell US treasuries in response to
President Trump's Greenland initiative,
Treasury Secretary Scott Besson
dismissed the idea as a false narrative,
stating at a press conference that it
defies any logic, and I could not
disagree more strongly on that. Even so,
Danish pension fund uh not gonna even
try to pronounce his name said Tuesday
it plans to exit US treasuries by month
end with chief investment officer
Andersh
telling Bloomberg uh the US is basically
not a good credit and long-term the US's
government finances are not sustainable.
We concur and reiterate our historic
call for investors to continue rotating
out of sovereign bond markets and into
gold. We've been saying since the summer
of 2023 that gold remains the best
diversifier away from the growing
geopolitically driven supply demand
imbalance in the Treasury bond market.
For what it's worth, uh, gold GLDM has
appreciated 144% since we altered the
theme. Meanwhile, Treasury bonds TLT
have returned only 1% on a total return
basis since then. Some serious alpha. As
always, members of our global investor
community can trust that our
institutional grade risk management
overlays, Kiss and Dr. will help our
portfolios navigate these emergent risks
better than investors attempting to
manage risk without systematic rules or
relying on fundamental research alone.

reality is uh I don't want to do that

have plenty of byside clients. Some of
the I'd say probably at least 15 of the

from an asset management standpoint are
clients of ours. Um, you know, so we're
happy to help them, but like I said,
when I started when we started this
company, you know, I didn't want to

rich people richer. I want to spend the

richer. Uh, to the extent that, you
know, you would continue to be
successful, uh, at the, uh, the research
process and in investment process that
we built. Uh, so getting back into the
question. So in my opinion, I think the
answer is really simple. Today is always
the best day to adjust your portfolio to
be on the right side of market risk. If
the systems that generate the uh the
gross exposure in the KISS process,
namely the uh the top down risk
management overlay, which we use our
marker regime downcasting process to
incorporate volatility targeting into
the system, and the bottom up management
overlay, we use our volatility
adjustable momentum signal for each
individual asset to incorporate dynamic
position sizing uh into the strategy. If
those systems are telling you that you
should be long 0% Bitcoin today, then
that's that's what the system is saying.
like there's no there should not be any
any debate. All the debate is about what
your fundamental belief system is. But
the reality is fundamental beliefs as
we've proven, you know, for the last
half decade, fundamental beliefs are
they're not as valuable in staying on
the right side of market risk as
quantitative risk management systems,
particularly institutional grade
quantitative risk management systems.
You know, we're in the middle of a
fourth turning poly crisis. your or my
or anybody's ability to use our puny
little human brains to successfully
navigate this from a portfolio
construction and asset allocation
standpoint is is lower than it normally
would be. You know, if this was the
1990s and all we had to worry about is a
productivity boom, it'd be a lot easier
to be an investor. But right now, we
it's a very difficult to be an investor
because we have so much stuff going on
from a geopolitical perspective. Um, in
terms of I mean I've been saying for
years the number one thing that's
driving all financial markets is this
growing geopolitically driven supply
demand imbalance in the treasury bond
market. It's the biggest market in the
world. If the biggest market in the
world has a supply demand imbalance,
it's going to reverberate across the
economy and all of global financial
markets. You know, in a way that has
caused gold to be up nearly 150% since
we authored the uh theme. U so in our
opinion, there's just too much stuff
going on as someone who does as much
fundamental research as any human being
on earth. And I guarantee that's true.
There's not a human being on earth that
pumps out hundreds of slides of research
every single week. Every single week I
pump out personally hundreds of slides
of data driven institutional grade
research. And I'm telling you right now,
as a person on earth who does the most
research on earth, there's no way I
could do what we're doing in terms of
creating these positive outcomes for our
clients without the use of institutional
grade systematic risk management
overlays. And so if those overlays are
telling you that right now you should be
long 0% Bitcoin, then
the debate's over. Any choice beyond
that is a choice to disregard the system
in a way that exposes your portfolio to
type two errors. Doesn't necessarily
guarantee that you're going to have a a
real hazardous outcome, but now you
officially exposed to potentially having
a type two error. The type two error is
when you reject a systematic signal. you
know, you're set you you reject the this
the call to take down risk into a
developing market crash and ultimately
the market crashes further. You know,
these are the types of errors that live
on the left side of the left tail of the
distribution. These are all the worst
outcomes that happen in financial
markets. You want to be short or not
participate in a raging bull market. You
want to be long in a raging bare market
type. Those are type two errors. And so
when you reject risk management six
signals like the ones we generate with
KISS and Dr. MO which is just KISS for
70 different factors for institutional
investors then you know you're exposing
yourself to you know the kinds of errors
that you know cause people to have
significantly worse lifestyles you know
and and and ultimately you know a lot of
times people you know they they they do
some dark stuff when they lose a lot of
money and so that's our goals here is to
eliminate the left t the left side of
the left tail of the distribution that's
exactly what the overlays and kiss and
Dr. uh do it at a great level. So, uh
you know, if you don't want to respond
and if you want to reject the signal,
